title: strychnos villosa
intro: Strychnos villosa, also known as the hairy strychnine tree or devil's bush, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. The medicinal properties of Strychnos villosa lie in its seeds, which contain strychnine, a toxic compound that has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of the plant include its potential use in treating fever, rheumatism, and snake bites, as well as its ability to act as a purgative and anthelmintic agent. Herbal preparations of Strychnos villosa include infusions, decoctions, and powder made from the seeds, which are traditionally used in traditional medicine practices.
